verses.text: At the second banquet the king again asks Esther's request, promising up to half the kingdom. The long-awaited moment has come. God has ripened it perfectly; what patience held back is now ready to be spoken.

At the second banquet the king again asks Esther's request, promising up to half the kingdom. The long-awaited moment has come. God has ripened it perfectly; what patience held back is now ready to be spoken.

Esther pleads for her own life and her people's, 'sold... to be destroyed, to be slain.' At last she speaks plainly. There is a time to be silent and a time to lay the whole truth bare before those who can act.

Esther pleads for her own life and her people's, 'sold... to be destroyed, to be slain.' At last she speaks plainly. There is a time to be silent and a time to lay the whole truth bare before those who can act.

'Who is he, and where?' the king demands; Esther answers, 'this wicked Haman,' and he is terrified before them. The accuser stands exposed. The plotter who feared no one now trembles, for his hidden malice has been dragged into the light.

'Who is he, and where?' the king demands; Esther answers, 'this wicked Haman,' and he is terrified before them. The accuser stands exposed. The plotter who feared no one now trembles, for his hidden malice has been dragged into the light.

The king storms out in wrath; Haman, begging Esther for his life, falls on her couch as the king returns, who takes it as assault, and they cover Haman's face. The condemned man's desperate act seals his doom. Sin, once uncovered, hurries the sinner from one ruin to the next.

The king storms out in wrath; Haman, begging Esther for his life, falls on her couch as the king returns, who takes it as assault, and they cover Haman's face. The condemned man's desperate act seals his doom. Sin, once uncovered, hurries the sinner from one ruin to the next.

Told of the gallows Haman built for Mordecai, the king orders, 'Hang him on it'; so Haman dies on his own gallows, and the king's wrath is calmed. The trap recoils on its maker. The pit dug for the righteous becomes the grave of the wicked.

Told of the gallows Haman built for Mordecai, the king orders, 'Hang him on it'; so Haman dies on his own gallows, and the king's wrath is calmed. The trap recoils on its maker. The pit dug for the righteous becomes the grave of the wicked.
